methods.method_name: Spatial Analysis of Digital Elevation Models
methods.description: This method involves the use of digital elevation models (DEMs) to analyze geomorphic features and reconstruct the extent and volume of eroded Quaternary alluvial fill deposits. It is used to determine the longitudinal river profile, terrace heights, and pre-incision surfaces for volume reconstruction.

steps.description: A 5 m DEM of the catchment, provided by the Hellenic Cadastre SA, was used to determine the longitudinal river profile and the heights of the terraces above the modern channel elevation.
steps.searchable_summary: use 5 m DEM to determine river profile and terrace heights
inputs: 5 m DEM from Hellenic Cadastre SA

steps.description: The DEM was also used to reconstruct the extent and volume of eroded Quaternary alluvial fill deposits.
steps.searchable_summary: reconstruct extent and volume of eroded deposits using DEM

steps.description: We used elevation data from mapped geomorphic units that exhibited little erosion to model a given deposit's pre-incision surface for the volume reconstruction.
steps.searchable_summary: model pre-incision surface using elevation data from stable geomorphic units
inputs: elevation data from stable geomorphic units

steps.description: These pre-incision surfaces were constructed via spline (regularised; weight 0.1) interpolation in ArcGIS.
steps.searchable_summary: construct pre-incision surfaces using spline interpolation in ArcGIS

steps.description: To determine eroded volumes and the original extent of the deposits, we subtracted the modern DEM from the interpolated pre-incision surfaces.
steps.searchable_summary: subtract modern DEM from interpolated surfaces to find eroded volumes

steps.description: A reconstruction of the pre-deposition valley bedrock morphology was created by subtracting the mapped thicknesses of individual deposits from the modern DEM.
steps.searchable_summary: reconstruct valley bedrock morphology by subtracting deposit thicknesses from DEM
